title: Longest Repeating Character Replacement
prompt: Given a string `s` and an integer `k`, you can change at most k characters to any other uppercase English letter. Return the length of the longest substring containing the same letter you can obtain.
constraints: s consists of uppercase English letters.
hints: A window is valid when (windowLen - mostFrequentCount) <= k.
Slide a right pointer; only shrink the window when invalid (move left).
You can keep mostFrequentCount as a non-decreasing 'lazy' max — it's correct for the answer.

optimal.approach: Sliding window with character-frequency array; track max-frequency in window.

pitfalls: Recomputing max across the alphabet on every step (still O(26n) — fine, but the lazy trick gives true O(n)).
followups: Allow at most k distinct characters (LC 340).
